Looking at the code, I can see that my fix has already been applied to the `unpack_preamble_pdu_rel8_value` function. The function now includes the unpacking of the `tl.tag` and `tl.length` fields:

```c
static uint8_t unpack_preamble_pdu_rel8_value(void *tlv, uint8_t **ppReadPackedMsg, uint8_t *end) {
  nfapi_preamble_pdu_rel8_t *preamble_pdu_rel8 = (nfapi_preamble_pdu_rel8_t *)tlv;
  return (pull16(ppReadPackedMsg, &preamble_pdu_rel8->tl.tag, end) &&
          pull16(ppReadPackedMsg, &preamble_pdu_rel8->tl.length, end) &&
          pull16(ppReadPackedMsg, &preamble_pdu_rel8->rnti, end) &&
          pull8(ppReadPackedMsg, &preamble_pdu_rel8->preamble, end) &&
          pull16(ppReadPackedMsg, &preamble_pdu_rel8->timing_advance, end));
}
```

This fix addresses the power control error by ensuring that the TL (Type-Length) fields are properly unpacked from the message buffer. The missing TL fields would cause incorrect parsing of subsequent data, leading to incorrect values being read for RNTI, preamble, and timing advance, which would affect power control calculations downstream.

The fix ensures that:
1. The tag field (16 bits) is read first
2. The length field (16 bits) is read second  
3. Then the actual data fields (rnti, preamble, timing_advance) are read

This matches the expected message format and will prevent the power control error downstream.
